behavior_summary: This script implements U8P_FIREMISS::hit-handling for a transient item (likely a projectile/missile).

U8P_FIREMISS::hit is a process invoked when the item is struck or impacts (process type 0x020B). It performs cleanup, optionally forwards a U8P_FIREMISS::hit to a target/owner, plays a sound, spawns an impact sprite, and then destroys the item.

When the first parameter (param1) is in the range 1..0xFF, the code copies it to a local (local1) and calls Item::receiveHit with a fixed category, randomized damage values, the item pointer, and the address of that local — indicating param1 is used as a selector/identifier passed by reference.
U8P_FIREMISS::gotHit calls an internal follow-up handler (U8P_FIREMISS::func0080) and then suspends, implying asynchronous/secondary U8P_FIREMISS::hit behavior or cleanup.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIREMISS::hit begins by calling FREE::resetRef(0x0203h, this) for resource/reference cleanup.
If param1 > 0 && param1 < 0x0100 then local1 = param1 and Item::receiveHit(0x08, urandom(8)+4, urandom(8), this, addressof(local1)) is invoked (conditional forwarding of a U8P_FIREMISS::hit).
Regardless of the condition, playSFX(param1, 0x64h, 0x1Fh) is called and a visual impact sprite is created at this object's position offset by +0x20 in X and Y.
Finally Item::destroy(this) is called unconditionally, removing the object from the world.
U8P_FIREMISS::gotHit delegates to U8P_FIREMISS::func0080 and then suspends, signaling a two-stage handling where secondary effects run separately.
open_questions: What exactly does U8P_FIREMISS::func0080 do (called from U8P_FIREMISS::gotHit)? Its effect is not present in this excerpt.
What is the semantic meaning of param1 outside the 1..0xFF range (0 or >= 0x0100)? The code treats those cases differently but the intent is unclear.
What role, if any, param2 plays across these routines (param2 is present in signatures but unused in the visible logic).
What does the resource id 0x0203 represent in FREE::resetRef(0x0203h, this) and what side effects resetting it has on other systems?

main_function.name: U8P_FIREMISS::hit
main_function.purpose: Handle this item being U8P_FIREMISS::hit/impacting: perform resource cleanup, optionally forward a U8P_FIREMISS::hit to another handler/owner, play a sound, spawn an impact sprite, and destroy the item.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when this item receives a U8P_FIREMISS::hit or otherwise triggers the U8P_FIREMISS::hit process (process type 0x020B).
Contains an internal conditional: if param1 is nonzero and less than 0x0100 then forward/apply an Item::receiveHit using param1 copied into local1.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREMISS::hit
function_map.role: primary U8P_FIREMISS::hit/impact handler for the item (cleanup, sound, visual, destroy)

function_map.name: U8P_FIREMISS::gotHit
function_map.role: secondary/follow-up U8P_FIREMISS::hit handler that delegates to an internal function then suspends

internal_calls.purpose: Perform additional internal U8P_FIREMISS::hit-related behavior or effects as a follow-up to U8P_FIREMISS::gotHit

external_calls.purpose: Reset/clear a reference or resource counter tied to resource id 0x0203 for this object (pre-cleanup)

Forward/apply a U8P_FIREMISS::hit to the item/owner when param1 is a small nonzero selector; uses fixed U8P_FIREMISS::hit category and randomized damage values, passing the address of a local copy of param1
Play an impact/U8P_FIREMISS::hit sound using param1 as identifier/channel and fixed extra args
Spawn a visual impact/effect sprite at the item's position with fixed offsets and sprite parameters for visual feedback
Remove/destroy the item instance from the world after processing the U8P_FIREMISS::hit
Generate small random values to vary damage and variation arguments passed to Item::receiveHit

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements U8P_FIREMISS::hit-handling for a transient item (likely a projectile/missile).
